findings.evidence: Line 44 states only `(1 ⊗ₜ a)`; Mathlib provides the underlying general tensor-product formula at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:69` as `lemma counit_tmul (x : A) (y : B)`, so the missing TauCeti-facing characteristic lemma is derivable but not exposed.
findings.fix: Replace or supplement it with a `[simp]` lemma for `(x : K)`, e.g. `Coalgebra.counit (R := K) (A := K ⊗[k] A) (x ⊗ₜ[k] a) = x * algebraMap k K (Coalgebra.counit a)` (up to the normal multiplication order), and optionally keep the `1 ⊗ a` specialization under a precise name.
findings.issue: `baseChange_counit_tmul` is named and advertised as the counit-on-pure-tensors API, but it only covers tensors of the form `1 ⊗ a`; it does not characterize `Coalgebra.counit` on a general `x ⊗ₜ[k] a`.

summary: The exposed base-change counit API is too special to characterize the counit on the base-changed Hopf algebra. Add the general pure-tensor normal form so downstream users do not have to unfold or rederive the tensor-product coalgebra instance.
